Patch Set 2:

I'm wondering if it might be better to make the change look more like upstream.

The gist of the upstream API is that all the timer users stop using the "unsigned long data" and use the from_timer() to get the object containing the timer.

From a backport perspective I think from_timer() and timer_setup() can be emulated in backports.

The only issue is the callback routine would need to be wrapped to add the now unused "unsigned long" as the last arg when indicated.

Patch Set 2:

All routine build and autotests do is confirm this change works on current versions.  Has it been tested at all on 4.15 to be sure it works correctly there?

Patch Set 2:

I have both server and client built against 4.15 and did a number of mdtests with a bunch eviction/recovery, they seem fine.
Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 2:

Donyang

James mentioned on the LWG call yesterday that he has not given a +1 to this patch because it conflicts with another patch that is about to land and will need to be rebased upon that. Unfortunately he did not mention the specific change but hopefully if you check master-next it will be obvious and you can rebase this change upon that one

Peter

Patch Set 2:

Their are two conflicts. One is the autoconf cleanup by Andreas that has landed already and the LU-9019 libcfs cleanup patch. I have a new rebased patch ready go to once the patches from master-next land to master proper.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

parents.subject: LU-10560 llite: remove assigning f_version in ll_readir.

commit.subject: LU-10805 libcfs: timer_setup() API changes
commit.message:
LU-10805 libcfs: timer_setup() API changes

Linux kernel 4.15 replaced setup_timer() with the new
interface timer_setup().
Introduce cfs wrappers to handle the API changes.

Linux-commit: e99e88a9d2b067465adaa9c111ada99a041bef9a

Signed-off-by: Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>
Change-Id: Ib79495f9ab7e955d6f72f1e9390cec0e23e2d641
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/31790
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@yahoo.com>
Reviewed-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
